questions.question: A train travels a distance of 480 km at a uniform speed. If the speed had been 8 km/h less, then it would have taken 3 hours more to cover the same distance. Find the usual speed of the train. (Note: Form a quadratic equation and solve it.)
questions.answer: Usual speed of the train = $40$ km/h

steps: Let the usual speed of the train be $x$ km/h.
Time taken at usual speed = distance/speed = $\frac{480}{x}$ hours.
If speed is 8 km/h less, new speed = $(x - 8)$ km/h.
Time taken at reduced speed = $\frac{480}{x - 8}$ hours.
According to the problem, the time at reduced speed is 3 hours more than the usual time: $\frac{480}{x - 8} = \frac{480}{x} + 3$.
Multiply both sides by $x(x - 8)$ to clear denominators: $480x = 480(x - 8) + 3x(x - 8)$.
Solve the quadratic equation: $x^2 - 8x - 1280 = 0$. Using quadratic formula $x = \frac{-b \pm \sqrt{b^2 - 4ac}}{2a}$ with $a=1$, $b=-8$, $c=-1280$.
Since speed cannot be negative, $x = 40$ km/h.
solution.derivation: Let usual speed = $x$ km/h. Usual time = $\frac{480}{x}$ h. Reduced speed = $(x-8)$ km/h. New time = $\frac{480}{x-8}$ h. Condition: $\frac{480}{x-8} = \frac{480}{x} + 3$. Multiply by $x(x-8)$: $480x = 480(x-8) + 3x(x-8)$. Simplify: $480x = 480x - 3840 + 3x^2 - 24x$. Cancel $480x$: $0 = 3x^2 - 24x - 3840$. Divide by 3: $x^2 - 8x - 1280 = 0$. Quadratic formula: $x = \frac{8 \pm \sqrt{64 + 5120}}{2} = \frac{8 \pm 72}{2}$. Positive root: $x = 40$.
